## Summary

All four evaluation output formats (webpage PDF, downloaded PDF, TXT, DOCX) now render **identical section structure** per the DREAM evaluation template. Previously each format had different content — DOCX was the most incomplete, and none included the enrichment surfaces from PR #976.

**Added to all three download formats (TXT, PDF, DOCX):**
- **Premise** — 1–2 sentence elevator pitch
- **Trigger Warnings** — content advisories with marketing recommendation
- **Reading Grade Level** — Flesch-Kincaid score + audience-appropriateness disclaimer
- **Dialogue vs. Narrative Ratio** — percentage breakdown with genre guidance

**Added to PDF + TXT downloads (were webpage-only):**
- Symbolic / Doctrine Audit (preserved symbols, doctrine strengths/risks, conclusion)
- Reader Experience (first act, middle, final act, aftertaste)
- Releasability (dimension table with verdict badges)

**Added to DOCX (was most incomplete):**
- Same three sections above
- These were already present in DOCX for Narrative Synthesis/Market Shelf/etc. via the dream block

**Governance removal:**
- Removed governance data loading (`getAllCanonGovernanceData`) from the download route entirely — no user (including admins) ever sees WAVE/Gate15/Golden Spine/Dialogue Canon in downloads

## Scope

- `app/api/reports/[jobId]/download/route.ts` — all three builder functions (`buildTxtReport`, `buildPdfReport`, `buildDocx`) + GET handler
- Does NOT touch the webpage rendering (`/reports/[jobId]/page.tsx`) which already has all sections
- Does NOT modify evaluation pipeline, scoring, or database

## Branch Freshness (Never Behind)

Branch-Behind-Base: 0

## Risks & Anomalies

- Enrichment data (premise, trigger warnings, reading grade level, dialogue ratio) is only available for evaluations run after PR #976 was deployed. Older evaluations will simply skip these sections gracefully (all guarded by `if (enrichment?.field != null)`)
- PDF releasability section uses coordinate positioning for verdict labels — tested that it renders correctly with pdfkit layout model

---

No-Pipeline-Impact: Confirmed — export-only changes, no evaluation logic modified
